site.office: King County District Court Judge · Position 1, Northeast Division
site.metaDescription: Bianca Tse is running for King County District Court Judge, Position 1, Northeast Division. Nearly 30 years as a Washington attorney, former King County prosecutor, and current pro tem judge — ready on day one.

hero.headline: Ready on Day One
hero.subhead: Bianca Tse is the only former King County prosecutor in this race — a Bellevue attorney with nearly 30 years in Washington courtrooms, ready to serve as a fair and thoughtful judge.

about.headline: Nearly 30 years in Washington courtrooms
about.body:
Bianca Tse has practiced law in Washington for nearly 30 years, most of that time right here in Bellevue. She's the only former King County Prosecutor running for King County District Judge.
For more than 16 years, she worked as a Deputy Prosecuting Attorney and tried dozens of cases in front of juries. She started with misdemeanor and felony cases in District and Superior Courts. She knows how a courtroom runs, knows the rules of evidence cold, and can think on her feet when a case takes a turn.
Today she's the Principal of her own firm, serving individuals, families, and small businesses across the Eastside. As a civil attorney, she's guided hundreds of clients through real estate deals worth millions. That's the same kind of civil work that comes before the District Court every day.
She carried heavy caseloads as a prosecutor, and she does the same now as a pro tem judge, working through high-volume calendars. She makes fair calls for defendants while keeping community safety in mind.
Bianca spent time in the Domestic Violence Unit, trying both felony and misdemeanor cases. She understands how domestic violence actually works. When someone comes to District Court seeking a protection order, she knows what's at stake and how to get the decision right.
She earned her law degree from Tulane and her undergraduate degree from the University of Washington. Her career comes down to a few things she believes: respect the law, treat people fairly, and make sure the justice system works for everyone, not just some.
She gives her time to the Eastside, too. She's a former board member and current development committee member of KidVantage, a local nonprofit that helps women and families in need. She's led a Girl Scout troop and volunteered in all kinds of roles at Bellevue schools.
about.photoCaption: Bianca with community members on the Eastside.

issues.headline: The experience this court needs
issues.intro: Every case matters. Everyone who comes through the courthouse doors deserves to be treated with respect and dignity — no matter why they are there.

items.title: Proven in Court
items.description: Nearly 30 years of legal practice in Washington. A former Deputy Prosecuting Attorney with both felony and misdemeanor trial experience, and a full command of evidence and trial procedure.

items.title: Community Roots
items.description: A trusted Bellevue attorney who has represented individuals, families, and small businesses across the Eastside, and gives her time to local schools and nonprofits.

items.title: Ready on Day One
items.description: Already serving as a pro tem judge at King County District Court and other Eastside municipal courts — prepared to step in and do the work from the first day on the bench.
